steps.title: Find the density increase
steps.content: The land area stayed the same, so the population increase equals the density increase times the land area. The density increase is $$3.0-2.3=0.7$$ people per square kilometer.

steps.title: Multiply by land area
steps.content: Multiply the increase in people per square kilometer by the number of square kilometers: $$0.7\cdot 91{,}600=64{,}120$$ The answer is D.
